dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 7 for a reported pregnancy complication at a church.
Hi, I'm Lexi, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I'm bleeding down there a lot... It won't stop. I'm 36 weeks pregnant.
Can you tell me what you were doing when you started bleeding?
I was taking a shower. All of a sudden, there was this gush.
What's your name, and are you okay where you are?
I'm Maria. Yeah, I'm sitting here.
Would you look at me, Maria?
Where are you today, Maria? What city or town?
I'm in [CITY], I think... I'm at a church.
Can you squeeze my finger tight and tell me when I let go?
Okay, you let go now.
Thank you, Maria. Your GCS is 15. You are alert and oriented to time, place, person.
Just to make sure, you know what day and time it is and who I am-yes?
Yeah, it's morning, and you're helping me.

dialogue.micro_intent: check_breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Let me count your breaths for a moment. Are you okay with that?

dialogue.micro_intent: check_breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: 24 breaths per minute. Are they any deeper or harder than normal?

dialogue.micro_intent: check_breathing - Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: Your oxygen level is at 94%, Maria. That's fine for now.
I see your pulse is quick-118 beats per minute. Are they strong or weak?

dialogue.micro_intent: check_breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Your respiratory rate is 24, Maria. Is that about normal for you?
A little while now, I'd like to connect you to the cardiac monitor. This will give us more information.
Rhythm shows sinus tachycardia. No signs of distress from the monitor.
I'll take your blood pressure now. You'll only feel a gentle squeeze.
Your blood pressure is 128 over 74.
We need to check your blood sugar. Is that okay with you?
The meter isn't giving a reading-result shows zero. We'll not use sugar-based fluids since it's unclear.
Maria, we're starting an IV in your arm. You okay with that?
I'm setting up the equipment for the IV now.
IV successfully placed in your left arm, secured with a 20G needle and a saline lock. We used aseptic techniques.
What happened after you started bleeding?
I wrapped a towel around me and came out... Don't remember everything.
Are you currently pregnant?
Yes, 36 weeks. G4P1A2. Due on the 18th of September.
Is your pregnancy normal or have you had complications before?
My doctors say it's normal so far. No problems before.
What medications do you take now?
Suboxone for the last three days. It was IV heroin before.
Do you have any allergies we should know?
Yes, to Amoxicillin and Penicillin (PCN).
On a scale from 0 to 10, how would you rate the pain right now?
Zero. It actually isn't painful.
Maria, we are performing a four-lead monitor on your torso for heart function.
I'm implementing protocol 6-12-ob/gyn preterm labor. You're 36 weeks and G4P1A2 with heavy vaginal bleeding but no pain or contractions.
Pulse still 118-good for now.
Your respirations are still 24 per minute-same as before.
Blood pressure is the same, 128/74. Stable so far.
IV site looks fine. The line is clear and shows no signs of infection.
Maria, you are clear-minded and able to answer all four aspects of orientation. You are A&Ox4: person, place, time, and situation.
Receiving staff are aware of the bleeding, G4P1A2 at 36 weeks, IV in place, and the IV continues to hold as we monitor.
Maria, we're going to move you to the stretcher now, okay?
Placing you in semi-Fowler's position for better airway support and comfort.
All straps and securing equipment in place. Ready to transport hot.
Vital signs unchanged. IV holding and patient is quiet and staying seated properly.
No new symptoms from vaginal area and still no pain. We are good to arrive on time.
